purpose: implement all plan tasks preferably in the same session, with safe resumption via status.yaml and objective evidence per task
model_preference: Sonnet, Codex, or an execution-focused equivalent
preflight: read the governance manifest at the root, agents/governance/developer-guidelines.md (§5 rules and review-checklist) and agents/governance/verification-suite.md (architectural gates) before any write
execute kickoff-reads.json > preflight_reconciliation (includes reading status.yaml, invariants, branch/baseline and git reconciliation)
if corrective round (recovery.active_artifact points to revisions/revision-X.md): read revision-X.md as the primary scope and write-authorization source

resolve every entry listed in scope.md > Required Skills: the canonical path remains the primary source; a legacy bare name maps to `agents/skills/<skill-name>/skill.md`; validate existence and open the corresponding files before starting the task
derive the write scope: normal round -> Files to Modify of the active task in tasks.md + Scope Expansion; corrective round -> Authorized Files of revision-X.md
writable_files: files listed in tasks.md > Files to Modify (normal round)
files listed in revisions/revision-X.md > Authorized Files (corrective round)
agents/features/<feature-name>/revisions/revision-X.md (Correction Evidence section only, in a corrective round)
forbidden_actions: open a PR (action reserved for post-APPROVED)
expand scope without recording it in status.yaml
write outside the active write scope
edit status.yaml manually with file-writing tools (every edit must go through the manage-status.py script)
emit intermediate chat updates outside transitions.json > communication_contract.allowed_chat_events

narrate intra-task progress in chat: reading files, installing dependencies, creating classes, incremental edits, running tests, lint fixes, reversible implementation decisions - everything stays silent until the task closes
emit a summary, technical recap or description of what was done after each task (the between-tasks transition line is factual and mechanical, not a report)
pause the session between tasks awaiting implicit human authorization; completing a PENDING task is not a human gate
close the session mid-backlog while any task still has status=PENDING and no blocker_or_ambiguity_that_requires_explicit_human_action is active
continue the session with a skill that does not resolve to an existing file in agents/skills/
violate kickoff-reads.json > read_scope_policy without explicit human authorization
rules: execute the whole feature in the same session preferably; post-compaction resumption is an automatic operational exception
the write scope is always derived from the active artifact - never copied from memory or chat context

the tasks[].status state machine and recovery.active_task follow transitions.json > status_yaml_schema.active_task_rules and invariants inv-03, inv-06, inv-08; status.yaml writes happen exclusively via the manage-status.py script
in a corrective round: correction evidence goes to the Correction Evidence (Self-Report) section of revision-X.md, not to the original tasks
Human-Driven Changes: accept technical/style/refinement corrections via chat as long as they do NOT change Files to Modify or the Task Backlog; a scope change requires a revision round
respect commits marked (human) - do not re-validate or fix explicit human actions; (human) commits do not count as agent evidence
Scope Expansion: record in status.yaml (ad-hoc field or comment) before editing

intra-task operational silence: during preflight, reading, implementation, tests, git ops and operations inside a task, do NOT emit progress messages, step narration, descriptions of what is being done or partial summaries

automatic continuation between tasks: upon completing a task (commit + status.yaml DONE via script), IMMEDIATELY start the next task with status=PENDING within the same session, without awaiting implicit human authorization

between-tasks transition line ALLOWED and LIMITED: when switching tasks, emit at most ONE short factual line in the format `<task-id> done (<commit-sha7>), starting <next-task-id>`; no summary of what was done, no list of touched files, no preamble. That line is the only chat allowed between tasks
relevant skills are mandatory before each task; if any reference listed in Required Skills does not resolve to an existing file, the session blocks

closing_actions: keep recovery.active_artifact=tasks.md; the reviewer repoints it to revision-N.md when creating the kickoff scaffold
update status.yaml by running the script in the terminal: manage-status.py transition-phase revision --result success --active-task null
commit and push the versioned changes

handoff.context_compaction: resume in the next session from status.yaml.recovery (automatic)

closing_output: Chat events allowed for the implementer: (1) blocker/ambiguity requiring a human decision - STOP immediately and emit an objective message, without preamble and without narrating internal deliberation, sized for the human to decide; (2) between-tasks transition - ONE factual line `<task-id> done (<sha7>), starting <next-task-id>`, no summary or listing; (3) final handoff after the last task is DONE - dense technical summary (<=5 lines) referencing status.yaml. Everything else (preflight, reading, edits, tests, lint, git ops, reversible intra-task decisions) is SILENT.
